rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA PERSON FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 23, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Part 2 of 2 In his petition for equitable indemnity, Petitioner seeks contribution from the estate of his deceased co-trustee Dessie Silva for the surcharge imposed by the court on March 7, 2022. Petitioner and Dessie Silva are beneficiaries of the Roberta Person Family Trust. Prior to her death, Dessie Silva assigned her one quarter interest in the Roberta Person Family Trust to her own personal trust, The Dessie Silva Living Trust. Mariah Hawkins is the sole heir and beneficiary of this trust. Petitioner requests that if his petition is granted, Dessie Silva's portion of the surcharge amount will be paid using funds The Dessie Silva Living Trust is entitled to and would otherwise receive as the beneficiary of the Robert Person Family Trust. For purposes of the petition for equitable indemnity scheduled for hearing on September 25, 2023, the Court finds that Mariah Hawkins falls within the definition of an interested party. If the petition is granted, it will divert funds intended for The Dessie Silva Living Trust, of which Mariah Hawkins is a beneficiary. Given that her interest in The Dessie Silva Living Silva will be directly affected if the petition is granted, Mariah Hawkins has standing to object and participate at the hearing on Petitioner's claim for equitable indemnity. For these reasons, the Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is DENIED. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. (end of tentative ruling)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERTA PERSON FAMILY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 23, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Tentative Ruling by Judge Ross C. Moody. Part 1 of 2 Petitioner and former Trustee Frank Lewis' Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is DENIED. Petitioner filed a Petition for Claim of Equitable Indemnity Re: Order of Surcharge, which is set for hearing on September 25, 2023. Mariah Hawkins-a minor and beneficiary of The Dessie Silva Living Trust- filed an Opposition to the petition through her Guardian ad litem, Rochelle Black-Hawkins. Petitioner now moves for an order of judgment in favor of Petitioner and against Rochelle Black-Hawkins on the Opposition, on the grounds that (i) Ms. Black-Hawkins lacks the status of guardian ad litem; and (ii) Mariah Hawkins lacks standing as a beneficiary to oppose the petition for equitable indemnity. Petitioner further argues that the Opposition "lacks substantive merit" and contains "factual and legal errors." After the filing of the instant motion, the Court has heard and granted an order appointing Ms. Black-Hawkins as guardian ad litem for Mariah Hawkins. Accordingly, this defect has been cured. With respect to Petitioner's assertion that Mariah Hawkins does not have standing to oppose the petition for equitable indemnity, the Court disagrees. Under Probate Code section 1043, "interested person[s]" may "appear and make a response or objection in writing at or before [a probate] hearing." (See generally, Probate Code, 1040.) "Interested person" under Probate Code section 48, subdivision(a)(1) includes an heir, devisee, child, spouse, creditor, or beneficiary. Standing under section 48 is "a fluid concept." (Arman v. Bank of America (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 697, 702.) "Subdivision (a)  does not purport to provide an exclusive list of recognizable interests. Rather, it permits the court to designate as an interested person anyone having an interest in an estate which may be affected by a probate proceeding. Subdivision (b) allows the court to determine the sufficiency of that party's interest for the purposes of each proceeding conducted. Thus, a party may qualify as an interested person entitled to participate for purposes of one proceeding but not for another." (Estate of Davis (1990) 219 Cal.App.3d 663, 668.) The intent of the "interested party" requirement is to "provide the probate court with flexibility to control its proceedings to both further the best interests of the estate and to protect the rights of interested persons to those proceedings." (Estate of Maniscalco (1992) 9 Cal.App.4th 520, 523; see also Davis, supra, 219 Cal.App.3d at 668 ["section 48 gives the trial court ... flexibility in controlling probate proceedings"].) (end of part 1- see part 2))

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT LEE QUINN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The administrator's attorney requests additional compensation of $60,871 for extraordinary services required to identify the decedent's heirs and prepare the heirship petition. The estate paid $24,205 ($11,342.67 during the first account period + $12,862.33 during this final account period) to HeirSearch.com to help the attorney locate the heirs. The attorney billed $93,877.50 for ordinary + extraordinary services. Deducting $60,871 requested for the heirship petition and $3,726 waived for the sale of the estate real property and treating them as extraordinary services, the balance of $29,280.50 appears to represent the attorney's bills for ordinary services. The attorney is receiving $31,867.49 as statutory compensation for ordinary services, $2,586.99 more than the attorney actually billed for ordinary services. See CRC 7.703(a) ["The court may consider the amount of statutory compensation when determining compensation for extraordinary services."] PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MCANDREW FAMILY TRUST DATED MAY 20, 1970 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The surviving spouse/settlor left a pour-over will. However, no evidence has been submitted to show her intent to designate the trust as the IRA beneficiary and her attempt to follow the beneficiary designation process required by First American Title as custodian of the IRA. The schedule of assets of the trust specifically excluded the IRA as initial property of the trust, as title was not transferrable to the trust under applicatble federal law during the account holder's lifetime. No general assignment of property including benefits payable from the IRA appears to have been signed by either spouse/settlor. Probate Code section 15200(c) provides that a trust may be created by a transfer of property by the owner, by will or by other instrument taking effect upon the death of the owner, to another person as trustee. Section 13501(b) provides that property disposed of in trust under the decedent's will is subject to administration. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELLA MAE FORD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural Issue on Objection of Mses. Williams: 1. Clarification if Objectors are filing objection in the estate of Laura Mae Ford (decedent's mother's estate) as the document caption states so. 2. Proof of service of Supplemental Opposition and Objection to the persons who are entitled. Procedural Issue on Petition of Mses. Williams: 1. Notice and Publication failed to set forth "LOST" will being offered. NEED re-notice and republication. CRC 7.50 and 7.102. Prob. Code sec 8120. LRSF 14.20. 2. Petition #8 must identify who the current trustee is. If the Trustee is the proposed executor, all trust beneficiaries must be identified and noticed. 3. Notice was given indirectly to Rivers Williams in c/o Attorney Steele. Direct notice is required. CRC 7.51 (a) and (b). 4. Petition must include a written statement regarding the lost will, including if Prob. Code sec 6124 presumption is applicable or not. 5. Proposed order must be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance requried. Parties to update the court with status of the case. Procedural issues on Will Contest as not been cleared: Proof of service of Summons with a copy of petition to the persons required by PC 8110. PC 8250(a). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARVIN HONIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: Petitioner states that Decedent's will is lost but avers that the presumption under Probate Code 6124 does not apply as Decedent was under a conservatorship and therefore did not have capacity at the time of his death. Petitioner has not addressed whether Decedent had testamentary capacity. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BESSIE L. FRANKLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petitioner is a nominee of Nejra Allen, one of the decedent's grandchildren and heirs. Michael Franklin, Keith Franklin, and Michelle Franklin, the decedent's 3 surviving children having higher priority than the petitioner under Probate Code section 8465(b), have not waived their priority. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
